nodes.context: Saigon street gang led by Đại Cathay, involved in prostitution, drugs, nightclubs, and casinos.
Chinese mafia in Chợ Lớn (Saigon's fifth district) led by Tín Mã Nàm, involved in illicit establishments.
One of the 'four great kings' of Saigon underworld, rival to Đại Cathay.
One of the 'four great kings' of Saigon underworld, rival to Đại Cathay.
One of the 'four great kings' of Saigon underworld, initially allied with Đại Cathay then turned rival.
Rival gang in Saigon's fourth district, fought with Đại Cathay for control.
Vietnamese militia that fought against the French; Đại Cathay's father served under them.

edges.context: Clashed violently in 1964; later negotiated a truce ceding territory.
Huỳnh Tỳ was one of the 'four great kings' who ambushed Đại Cathay.
Ngô Văn Cái was one of the 'four great kings' who ambushed Đại Cathay.
Ba Thế initially allied with Đại Cathay in the attack on Tín Mã Nàm, but later joined the ambush against him.
Fought for control of the fourth district after Đại Cathay returned from reformatory.
After negotiation, Tín Mã Nàm ceded territory to Đại Cathay, allowing him to operate casinos.
Ba Thế joined Đại Cathay in the attack on Tín Mã Nàm's gang.
